page: Amazon Web Services r6g.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance powered by 64 physical cores of the 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton processor (Neoverse-N1) running at 2.5 GHz. It features a dedicated CPU allocation with one thread per core, 32 MB of L3 cache, and 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals strong multi-core and memory subsystem capabilities. The server achieves top-tier performance in multi-core CPU stress tests, cached small-block memory read bandwidth (3792.76 GB/sec), and software compilation. It also demonstrates high throughput in Redis SET operations and static web serving. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with weak results in single-threaded gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench single-core tests. Large LLM inference workloads also show poor text generation speeds.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture delivers high resource density and memory capacity relative to its power profile, making it a viable alternative to x86 bare-metal instances for memory-intensive workloads. The server is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases like Redis, high-throughput web serving, multi-threaded software compilation, and memory-heavy containerized applications. It is less suitable for single-threaded legacy applications or heavy LLM text generation tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r6g.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton processor (Neoverse-N1) running at 2.5 GHz. It features 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, alongside a baseline network speed of 25 Gbps. No local storage or GPU hardware is included. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core processing, high cached memory read bandwidth, and exceptional Redis and static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds represent clear tradeoffs, scoring in the lower percentiles. Qualitatively, the ARM64 architecture offers excellent resource density and memory capacity relative to its hardware class. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, multi-threaded software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6g.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized ARM64 server with 64 dedicated physical cores, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for in-memory databases.

tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized ARM64 platform delivering 512 GB of RAM and 64 dedicated physical cores for memory-intensive workloads.

512.0 GB of DDR4 memory (8.0 GB per core)
Strong multi-core CPU and cached memory read performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for in-memory databases, web serving, and software compilation
